title: thesium ramosum var. divaricatum
intro: Thesium ramosum var. divaricatum, also known as the 'divaricate thyme-leaved marigole', is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae, characterized by its thyme-like leaves and marigold-like flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Thesium ramosum var. divaricatum include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe wounds, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared as an infusion or tincture, and it is also used in combination with other herbs to create topical creams and salves for skin conditions and wound healing.
